response: TheThe Cold War saw the United States and the Soviet Union lead their respective blocs. The United States had presidents like John F. Kennedy and Ronald Reagan. The Soviet Union had leaders like Nikita Khrushchev and Mikhail Gorbachev. The conflict lasted for decades with intense tension. The two superpowers competed globally. The world lived in fear of nuclear war. The end came when the Soviet Union collapsed. The United States emerged as the sole superpower. The era defined modern history. The struggle shaped international relations. The tension was constant. The threat was real. The time was dark. The future was uncertain. The past is now history. The story is told.
